summary: Updated the progress-reporting helper `tools/report_score.py` to work with the dtk-era build layout. The script now accepts a source file path instead of an object path, derives the corresponding `.o` path with `Path.with_suffix`, and reads expected/WIP objects from `build/GALE01/obj` and `build/GALE01/src` rather than the older `expected/build/ssbm.us.1.2` and `build/wip/ssbm.us.1.2` roots.
what_changed: The CLI positional argument changed from `obj_path` as a string to `src_path` as a `Path`.
`main` now treats the input as a source path and derives the object path via `src_path.with_suffix(".o")`.
Report title generation now uses `src_path.stem` for the short title.
Full report heading now prints the source path instead of the previous object-name-relative-to-expected-root value.
The argparse description changed from a placeholder integer-processing message to `Report progress of a translation unit`.
smart_moves: Switched the user-facing input to the source file path, which matches how decomp work is usually discussed and avoids requiring contributors to manually map source files to object paths.
Derived the object path mechanically with `Path.with_suffix(".o")`, reducing duplicated knowledge about source/object naming.
Moved hardcoded roots to the dtk-style `build/GALE01` layout, keeping the scoring tool aligned with the current build system.
Improved CLI help text so the script describes its real purpose: reporting progress for a translation unit.
decomp_lessons: When build systems or object layouts change, ancillary scoring/reporting tools need to be updated promptly so progress metrics remain usable.
For decomp workflow tools, prefer source-file-oriented CLI inputs where possible; source paths are more natural for contributors and can be converted to object paths internally.
Use `pathlib.Path` transformations such as `with_suffix` for source-to-object path derivation instead of string replacement when practical.
Hardcoded build roots in tooling should reflect the active build layout; stale paths can silently make otherwise useful progress tools unusable.
naming_conventions: The script uses `src_path` for the user-supplied source translation unit path.
The derived object path is named `obj_path`.
The dtk build roots are represented as `expected_root = Path("build/GALE01/obj")` and `wip_root = Path("build/GALE01/src")`.
Title-only output uses `src_path.stem` as the friendly translation-unit name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic was introduced; the change supports matching work indirectly by keeping progress-score reporting functional under the dtk build layout.
The script continues to compare expected and WIP object outputs and report file/function score percentages, but the diff excerpt only shows path/input reconfiguration.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further modify `tools/report_score.py` after the dtk build layout migration.
Check whether other tools still reference `expected/build/ssbm.us.1.2` or `build/wip/ssbm.us.1.2` after PR 1100.
Search for usage examples of `report_score.py` to confirm expected `src_path` invocation patterns.
